event: Portrait of Saint Benedict of Nursia
symbols: broken or cracked cup — the legend of poison revealed and rendered harmless by the sign of the cross
black habit — the Benedictine monastic order founded by the saint
rocky wilderness landscape — the ascetic withdrawal from worldly life
small figure in background — a fellow monk or disciple in the monastic community
description: A full-length portrait of a tall, gaunt man in a voluminous black Benedictine monk's habit standing against a rocky, mountainous landscape. He has a tonsured head and an austere, contemplative expression. He holds a white ceramic cup or vessel in both hands, slightly raised before him. The rocky terrain behind features dark crags and a moody clouded sky. A small dark-robed figure is visible in the lower left background. The palette is dominated by the deep black of the habit against warm browns and greys of the landscape.
